definition: ROIC-WACC spread compression from ~6-8pp (2020-21) to ~4-5pp currently creates nonlinear equity sensitivity to rate changes. At a 4pp spread, a 1pp WACC increase destroys ~25% of economic profit vs ~10% at a 10pp spread, making the equity market structurally more rate-sensitive.
evidence: Pair_1 debate rated this 'genuinely useful reframing'
Rates now damage equity fundamentals (earnings) not just valuations (P/E)
Next equity downturn potentially more rate-sensitive than any since 1970s
open_questions: Whether mega-cap tech with high ROIC is immune to this dynamic
How quickly ROIC-WACC spreads can re-expand in a rate-cutting cycle
